name: SYN-RTOS pre-V3 releng scaffold (not independently named by user; earliest repo-tracked archiso profile)
desktop_stack: none - stock archiso releng, no desktop overlay; a parallel 'xibo-build' kiosk profile exists as a sibling top-level dir at this same commit but is explicitly excluded from this manifest per user instruction (inconsequential client-project lint, not a real edition)
trajectory: First real committed profile in the whole project (repo created 2021-03-02). 'releng' is barely-modified stock archiso releng (autologin, ssh, reflector tweaks) at 102 packages, no desktop/dotfiles yet. Never independently develops further as its own line - directly superseded by the real V3 desktop/dotfile work below (SYN-RTOS-ARCHISOFILES). Per the user's own V1/V2/V3 numbering (V1/V2 = pre-repo manual Arch use, not applicable; V3 = first real desktop/dotfiles work), this releng scaffold sits BEFORE the true V3 milestone - it is included here only because it is the literal first commit-tracked artifact, not because it satisfies the user's V3 definition on its own.
notes: profiledef.sh and packages.x86_64 confirmed as real siblings via git ls-tree. Real substance (102 pkgs) despite being close to vanilla. The xibo-build/ sibling at this same commit is real desktop/kiosk content (chromium fullscreen) but per user's explicit instruction is NOT given its own entry, and is also not what satisfies the real V3 definition - that is SYN-RTOS-ARCHISOFILES (next entry), where desktop/dotfiles genuinely show up.

name: SYN-RTOS-V3 (SYN-RTOS-ARCHISOFILES, Openbox+Tint2 era - the real first desktop/dotfiles milestone)
desktop_stack: Xorg + Openbox + tint2 panel, plus a grab-bag of KDE apps (kwrite, kdenlive, spectacle - later individually deleted in Nov 2022 cleanup) and gtk apps (pcmanfm-qt, qBittorrent, VLC, ranger)
trajectory: packages.x86_64 created once at 'Major Changes' (ff4c3e4, 2022-11-08) at 140 packages and NEVER edited again for the rest of rtos/main history (confirmed via full git log on the path - only 1 commit touches packages.x86_64 in the whole SYN-RTOS-ARCHISOFILES lifetime). However the surrounding profile (39 total commits touching the dir) kept receiving real overlay/theme/pacman.conf/stage0-script work through to the rtos/main branch tip on 2023-02-11 (Openbox themes added, tint2 configs added/replaced, stale KDE app configs deleted, vconsole.conf added, pacman-key fixes). So: package list frozen at 140 but the profile as a whole was actively developed right up to the SYN-OS handoff. This is the real V3 (stage0/stage1 split era: syn-stage0.sh multiple updates, syn-stage1.sh, syn-stage00.sh/syn-stage11.sh created 2023-01-16 as the last rtos/main commits before handoff).
notes: Old V1/V2 material (syn-installer0/1.sh, the original kiosk-adjacent scripts) is archived INSIDE this profile's overlay at 'SYN-RTOS-ARCHISOFILES/airootfs/root/SYN-RTOS-V3/0.Legacy V1_V2 Files/' - confirming the user's V1/V2/V3 numbering is literally encoded in the real directory name '1.root_filesystem_overlay' vs '0.Legacy V1_V2 Files'. profiledef.sh + packages.x86_64 + airootfs confirmed as real top-level siblings. This is the terminal state of rtos/main (repo ends 2023-02-11); SYN-OS work begins the same day on the main (SYN-OS-Archive) repo.

name: SYN-OS initial consolidation (RAW baseline + SYN-OS-ARCHISO_PROFILE stub)
desktop_stack: none - vanilla archiso releng baseline only (base, cloud-init, hyperv, linux, mkinitcpio, open-vm-tools, openssh, virtualbox-guest-utils-nox)
trajectory: Created same day as SYN-RTOS-ARCHISOFILES's terminal state, message 'SYN-RTOS and SYN-OS Profiles RAW. A basic version of the baseline package offered by Archiso.' packages.x86_64 touched at creation (8ff0119) and then only ONE more time at 7e1e0cd (2024-10-28) which is purely a path-rename into 'Older versions/' during the Oct 2024 reorg, not a real edit - confirmed via full git log walk on the path (only 2 commits total, second is the archival move). Genuinely frozen at 12 packages for its entire ~20-month life in the tree, deleted from the working tree (moved to fully-inert 'Older versions/') at 7e1e0cd, then the whole 'Older versions/' archive was deleted from main at commit 9df0f28a7fe5c5203fcedad64d501e1034216d19 (2026-02-15).
notes: CONFIRMS the earlier same-session finding: this is a genuine stub, never developed beyond its initial vanilla-releng scaffold. Real siblings confirmed via ls-tree (airootfs, packages.x86_64, profiledef.sh, etc. all present). Do not confuse with SYN-OS-V4 or SYN-OS-2035, both of which are real full editions created around the same time and initially sat as siblings under the same parent commit.

name: SYN-RTOS-OLD (frozen V3 snapshot carried into SYN-OS repo)
desktop_stack: Xorg + Openbox + tint2 (identical to SYN-RTOS-V3 above - this is a direct import of that tree's final state, not new work)
trajectory: This is literally the final rtos/main SYN-RTOS-ARCHISOFILES tree, re-imported wholesale into the new SYN-OS repo as a reference/legacy snapshot during the 'SYN-OS CONSOLIDATION' reorg. packages.x86_64 touched only at creation and once more at the 7e1e0cd path-rename into 'Older versions/' (2024-10-28) - never independently developed inside the SYN-OS repo. Archive deleted from main tree entirely at 9df0f28 (2026-02-15).
notes: This is one of the two 'parallel profiles' the earlier rushed check correctly identified as frozen/duplicate - confirmed here with a full-range walk, not just a single sample point. Real content (140 pkgs, real Openbox+tint2 desktop) but zero independent development after import - a pure legacy artifact kept for reference, matching the user's own directory name 'SYN-OS-V3 or Earlier'.

name: SYN-OS-V4
desktop_stack: Xorg + Openbox + KDE apps (dotfiles kept separately at SYN-OS-V4/SYN-DOTFILES/, not baked into airootfs - includes kwrite, spectacle, pcmanfm-qt, qBittorrent, kitty terminal). No panel app (tint2/polybar) confirmed present in the dotfile set at inspection time.
trajectory: Created 2023-06-05 at 123 packages (matches user's prior confirmed figure). packages.x86_64 touched again once more at 7e1e0cd (2023-06-05 create, then only one more touch at 7e1e0cd 2024-10-28) - i.e. genuinely frozen at 123 packages for its entire real life, confirmed via full git log walk (only 2 commits touch the path total). The directory persisted UNCHANGED in the live tree from 2023-06-05 through 2024-07-08 (df1e37c, last commit where it's still present as a top-level dir) - over a year of dormancy while SYN-OS-2035 and later SYN-OS/SYN-ISO-PROFILE absorbed active development. Archived into 'Older versions/SYN-OS-V4' at 7e1e0cd (2024-10-28), then the whole 'Older versions/' tree deleted at 9df0f28 (2026-02-15).
notes: This is the SECOND of the 'parallel profiles' correctly flagged by the earlier check - genuinely frozen, confirmed here with full-range verification. It sat alongside SYN-OS-2035 as a sibling from creation, but 2035 (below) is where real forward development actually happened; V4 was effectively abandoned in place rather than actively developed. The 'SYN-OS CONSOLIDATION' commit series (SYN-BUILDER, SYN-GRAPHER, SYN-TOOLS) that created V4 also created significant tooling (dir-graphing scripts, rebuild scripts) that lived under SYN-OS-V4/SYN-TOOLS/ - real engineering effort went into the *tooling* even though the package list itself never grew.

name: SYN-OS-2035 (pre-rename phase - genuine 128-package launch)
desktop_stack: Xorg + Openbox + tint2 (SYN-RTOS-DARKGREEN/DARKRED/TEMPLATE tint2rc themes carried over), oh-my-zsh shell framework, KDE/gtk apps similar to V3/V4 lineage
trajectory: THE ALREADY-KNOWN CORRECTED GAP FROM EARLIER TONIGHT, now fully re-verified with a complete commit-by-commit walk (14 commits checked individually with git show for exact package counts, not just message text). Dotfile staging began 2023-09-23 (b1a03e4, no profiledef.sh yet). Real buildable profile launched 2023-09-25 at fae5b43 with 128 packages - genuine substance, not a stub. Package count then held FLAT at exactly 128 for 4 months across 8 more commits (8d60392, 8f00d8f, 323c98b, 68578af, 05fd4f7, b585ef2, 7d8c468, 4125bd0 - spanning 2023-09-25 to 2024-01-22) despite commit messages suggesting major activity ('Seriously improved the ISO build process', 'Early stages of LUKS', 'Next Season' x2, 'Cannot Build ISO due to GPL2 files not being found'). This confirms the user's stated suspicion: vague/optimistic commit messages during this window did NOT correspond to package-list growth - the real work in this window was boot-process/LUKS/GPL-license-cleanup plumbing, not package expansion, and by 4125bd0 the profile could not even build ('Cannot Build ISO due to GPL2 files not being found').
notes: iso_version string in profiledef.sh at this phase is literally "SYN-OS 2035" / "2035" - directly confirms the branding in code, not just directory naming. This phase ends in a real crisis (unbuildable ISO due to GPL2 license issue) which directly precedes the 'Starting again from baseline' resets below.

name: SYN-OS-2035 (reset phase - 'Starting again from baseline')
desktop_stack: unknown/minimal at reset point - package count crash to 12 suggests a fresh archiso baseline re-scaffold, desktop overlay not re-verified at this exact narrow window since it is superseded within 24-48 hours
trajectory: Confirms via real package-count data (not just message text) that 'Starting again from baseline' (appearing 3x: dd75fe5, bef8d81, 31b9b0b, all within 2024-01-27 to 2024-01-28) was a REAL reset, not vague/empty language: package count crashed from 128 (held flat for 4 months) down to 12 at dd75fe5, then climbed back to 30 by bef8d81/31b9b0b (same day, 2024-01-28). This is a genuine rebuild-from-scratch event, exactly the kind of real data-loss-adjacent event the user suspected might exist in the 2022-2024 window - EXCEPT in this case it is fully explained and intentional (the prior GPL2-license-blocked build at 4125bd0 was unbuildable, so a clean re-scaffold was the correct fix, not accidental loss).
notes: This narrow 2-day window is the direct bridge between the 128-package 2035 launch and the 'Brand New Archiso as Baseline' rename below. Worth keeping as its own entry because the package-count crash is real and dramatic and a builder script or download page should NOT treat 2024-01-22 through 2024-01-28 as a coherent single state - there were two very different real package counts (128 then 12) within days of each other.

name: Chronomorph (SYN-OS/SYN-ISO-PROFILE launch - first working end-to-end ISO)
desktop_stack: Xorg + Openbox (early minimal baseline at this exact point; polybar/panel apps confirmed present by the later XENITH sample point, exact date polybar was added within this range not individually pinpointed)
trajectory: 'Brand New Archiso as Baseline' (068459f, 2024-01-28) creates SYN-OS/SYN-ISO-PROFILE as an entirely new path at 30 packages, ONE DAY AFTER the SYN-OS-2035 reset above - this is effectively the same rebuild continuing under a new path/name, not an unrelated new project (both events are 2024-01-27/28, immediately sequential). iso_version string corrected from "2035" to "chronomorph" the same day (58ea95c, 'Version correction - chronomorph') confirming this in code. 'Download ISO available' commit at 563903e (2024-02-19) marks the first real public release under this name. Package count grew slowly: 30 (068459f) -> 35 (ac58781, 2024-01-29) -> 38 (40822c4, 2024-02-09) -> 37 (aa56956, 2024-05-02) -> 38 (aff485a, 2024-05-06) before the SOAM-DO-HUAWEI rename below.
notes: IMPORTANT PATH NOTE: SYN-OS-2035/SYN-ISO-PROFILE (128->12->30 pkgs) and SYN-OS/SYN-ISO-PROFILE (30 pkgs onward) briefly COEXISTED in the tree simultaneously at 068459f - the old 2035 profile was NOT deleted immediately, it lingered untouched at 30 packages until being archived at 7e1e0cd (2024-10-28, see 2035 entries above). Builder scripts must key off the SYN-OS/SYN-ISO-PROFILE path specifically for this era onward, not SYN-OS-2035. This SYN-OS/SYN-ISO-PROFILE path is the same lineage that continues, uninterrupted, all the way to the current tip of main today.

name: SOAM-DO-HUAWEI
desktop_stack: Xorg + Openbox (same lineage/continuity as Chronomorph, no major desktop stack change identified at this specific commit)
trajectory: iso_version string changes from "chronomorph" to "SOAM-DO-HUAWEI" at this exact commit ('fixed motd and login info and prompts info', 2024-05-04). Package count essentially flat (37, down slightly from Chronomorph's 38) - this looks like primarily a branding/motd change rather than a functional milestone. Superseded 7 days later by VOLITION.
notes: Very short-lived named era (7 days, 2024-05-04 to 2024-05-11) confirmed only by the profiledef.sh iso_version string, not by any substantial package or desktop-stack change. A builder/download page should probably treat this as a minor point release rather than a headline edition, though it is real (present in the README's own release table per the reconstructed-root index) and gets its own Google Drive download link dated May 2024.

name: VOLITION
desktop_stack: Xorg + Openbox; browser swapped chromium->qutebrowser (2024-07-07, c4e26ec) ->vivaldi (2024-07-08, d737165) within this era
trajectory: iso_version becomes "VOLITION" at e1fa873 (2024-05-11, 'Solved qt5 glitch making huge windows'), 38 packages. The 'ArchTech Corp Edition' ISO upload (7b55f0f, 2024-07-08) is a distribution event on this same codebase, not a separate named era the user cares about - superseded by M-141 as the next real headline edition. VOLITION as a code-version string persists until 7e1e0cd (2024-10-28) when profiledef.sh switches to a dynamic $(date +%Y.%m.%d) version scheme.
notes: ArchTech Corp Edition intentionally not given its own entry per user direction ('forget archtech if that's not useful') - it's a real upload but not one of the named editions worth surfacing on a download page.

name: M-141
desktop_stack: Xorg + Openbox; MBR/UEFI installer split introduced this commit (syn-stage0.zsh/syn-stage1.zsh separated cleanly, two-script model)
trajectory: profiledef.sh iso_version switches from the literal string "VOLITION" to a dynamic $(date +%Y.%m.%d) expression at 7e1e0cd - meaning no subsequent era has a fixed iso_version string burned into the build config; naming from here on (M-141, SYNTEX, XENITH, SYNAPTICS, AEGIS) lives in README prose/commit messages/download-link labels, not the build artifact's version field. Same commit archives SYN-OS-2035, SYN-OS-V4, and 'SYN-OS-V3 or Earlier' into 'Older versions/'. Per the user directly: M-141 WAS the real, actively-used named edition of this period - the daily-driver build they were actually running, not a relabel. The repo itself stays quiet through this window (package count flat at 39 from 2024-10-28 to 2025-01-04) because the real day-to-day work was happening locally on the user's main machine, not being committed incrementally.
notes: Represents the codebase as of 7e1e0cd, which is the real commit underlying the M-141 period even though most of what made M-141 'M-141' in daily use (config tweaks, live customization) never made it into a discrete commit. Builder script should label this state "M-141" using this commit, understanding it's the closest real buildable anchor for that named edition, not a byte-for-byte guarantee of everything the user ran locally at the time.

name: SYNTEX
desktop_stack: Xorg + Openbox (still pre-Wayland at this point; Graphviz documentation/diagramming tooling added this era, not a desktop-stack change)
trajectory: README table places 'SYN-OS SYNTEX (April 2025)'; the real commit with SYNTEX in its message is 078920f, 2025-08-25. Per the user directly: this gap is real and explained, not a data-integrity problem - they were running/editing M-141 on their main machine throughout this window (procrastinating on committing), so the repo's own quiet stretch (261be98, 2025-01-04, through 1e73887/27f4552/5824bff, all 2025-08-14) reflects uncommitted local iteration, not lost work or an unexplained skip. The April 2025 date in the README is the user's own approximate memory of when SYNTEX-era changes were actually happening locally, ahead of when they got committed.
notes: The '2025 pre 2035 update' commits (1e73887, 27f4552, both 2025-08-14) were checked via diff: NOT a return to the old SYN-OS-2035 tree, just an in-place packages.x86_64 rewrite that briefly showed 0 lines mid-edit before recovering same day - confirmed real, not data loss. Bridges cleanly now that the user's own account (mainbox edits under M-141, committed later as SYNTEX) explains the timeline.

name: XENITH
desktop_stack: Xorg + Openbox + Polybar (confirmed via real dotfile-overlay inspection: airootfs/root/syn-resources/DotfileOverlay/etc/skel/.config/polybar present; multiple stale Openbox theme sets present too - w-lbobtp2-pancake, w-lbobtp2-paralellogram, w-lbobtp2-tiefighter per commit message, real cleanup of years-old theme cruft)
trajectory: Commit message itself is explicit and self-aware about the gap: 'Over 8 or 9 months of various building' (2026-01-29), covering roughly the SYNTEX->XENITH silent window, with over 1600 files changed in one large commit (real changes to syn-disk-config.zsh, syn-packages.zsh, syn-stage1.zsh alongside theme cleanup). Package count 46 (SYNTEX) -> 48 here. 'MAJOR UPDATES' follow-up at d884a7d (2026-01-31).
notes: This is a real, large, substantive commit (not just relabeling) - confirmed via the DotfileOverlay path structure showing genuine Polybar+Openbox theme work. The 'silence' in the commit log between SYNTEX and XENITH is explained by the user's own established pattern (real work happens, gets committed in one large batch rather than incrementally) rather than data loss - consistent with the 'Next Season' pattern from 2023 SYN-OS-2035 era. Package path is still airootfs/root/syn-resources/... at this point (pre the later usr/lib/syn-os/ relocation seen at current tip).

name: SYNAPTICS (Full Wayland Move)
desktop_stack: Wayland + labwc + waybar (confirmed via dotfile overlay: .config/labwc and .config/waybar present, replacing Openbox/Polybar; legacy Openbox theme dirs under usr/share/themes/ retained for compatibility but no longer the active WM)
trajectory: 'Early upgrades to wayland - incomplete -labwc and waybar coming soon' (751d13a, 2026-02-08) is the honest incomplete-state commit, followed the next day by 4 commits all titled 'Full Wayland Move / Synaptics Upload' (fb35b1d, d0d363b, f6e3221, 1703d09, all 2026-02-09) which complete the transition. Package count 48 (XENITH) -> 50. This confirms the SYNAPTICS naming refers specifically to the Wayland/labwc/waybar migration, matching the desktop-stack evidence directly rather than being an arbitrary label.
notes: Multiple same-day, same-message commits (4x 'Full Wayland Move / Synaptics Upload') suggest an iterative same-day push rather than 4 distinct milestones - treat 1703d09 (last of the 4) as the representative commit for this state. This is also the point where the desktop stack the CURRENT SYN-OS uses (LabWC/Waybar/Foot per the live readme.md) was first established - SYNAPTICS is the direct ancestor of the present-day desktop, not a dead-end branch.

name: AEGIS (pre-Aegis / Aegis / dropped)
desktop_stack: Wayland + labwc + waybar (same lineage as SYNAPTICS, continuing forward - no desktop-stack regression, this is a branding/maturity milestone not a technical rebuild)
trajectory: 'pre-aegis upload for iso - build files tested and ready' (1989434, 2026-02-24, 51 pkgs) -> 'Pre-Aegis - Lots changed - Readme needs updating' (9c8875d, 2026-03-02, 51 pkgs) -> 'Aegis readme and project in a good state' (e639a93, 2026-03-03, 51 pkgs) -> (a later 'Ageis' typo-fix commit 6f2a7f3 exists per reconstructed-root reference, real commit not independently re-verified here but consistent with the pattern) -> 'drop AEGIS branding, add full project docs with Graphviz diagrams' (f1e9cf2, 2026-07-10, 51 pkgs). AEGIS as a branded name spans roughly 2026-02-24 to 2026-07-10, then is explicitly retired: profiledef.sh iso_name/os-release revert to plain 'SYN-OS' per the f1e9cf2 commit message itself, which explicitly states this follows 'the pattern of every prior era eventually shedding its release name once superseded.'
notes: AEGIS is the most recently completed named era and the first one the project's own commit messages explicitly self-document as part of a recurring pattern (name adopted, then dropped once superseded) - this is useful corroboration that the naming churn is a known, deliberate project habit, not confusion. f1e9cf2 also adds docs/history.md and docs/packages.md as real documentation-of-record, generated from git log/git show across both repos per its own commit message - i.e. this codebase already contains a prior good-faith attempt at exactly this kind of historical reconstruction, worth cross-checking against for consistency.

name: Current / present (unbranded SYN-OS, post-AEGIS)
desktop_stack: Wayland + labwc + waybar + swaybg + wmenu + foot (terminal) + spf/Superfile (file manager) + zsh (autosuggestions/syntax-highlighting/fuzzy search) - per the live readme.md's own component table, cross-checked against real dotfile-overlay paths (airootfs/usr/lib/syn-os/DotfileOverlay/etc/skel/.config/{labwc,waybar})
trajectory: This is the tip of main as of this research session (2026-07-12 per system date). packages.x86_64 has grown to 52 real lines, but this UNDERCOUNTS the true installed-system package total: docs/packages.md (added f1e9cf2) documents 81 packages total per the commit's own message, because packages.x86_64 in this repo layout is only the LIVE-ISO bootstrap set (base, linux, filesystem tools, network tools, lvm2/cryptsetup/btrfs-progs/xfsprogs for the installer's partition strategies) - the actual desktop/application package set (waybar, foot, superfile, browser, media player, image editor etc.) is installed post-boot by the syn-stage1/syn-packages.zsh install script, not baked into the ISO's own packages.x86_64. grub/ dir removed from the profile at 9df0f28 (2026-02-15, 'removed grub for bootctl') - confirms the project moved to bootctl/systemd-boot only, dropping GRUB entirely; also the 'Older versions/' full archive (2035, V4, V3-or-earlier) was deleted from the tree in that same commit.
notes: IMPORTANT PACKAGE-COUNT CAVEAT for any download-page/builder script: package_count here (52) is NOT comparable apples-to-apples with the counts reported for V3/V4/2035-era editions, which baked their full desktop package sets directly into a single packages.x86_64 for the ISO itself. This repo's current architecture deliberately splits bootstrap packages (in packages.x86_64) from desktop/application packages (installed live by script), so 52 is real but represents a different scope than e.g. V3's 140. Use docs/packages.md's 81-package figure (if it needs to be re-verified independently) for an apples-to-apples 'full installed system' comparison, not packages.x86_64 alone. This entry represents the SYN-OS-Archive.git bare mirror's real main tip, which is the same commit family the SYN-OS-Reconstructed worktree used as its source material tonight - but the Reconstructed branch is a re-authored history built FROM this, not identical to it commit-for-commit.
